## Highlights

- Added OpenCode Console API billing support, including configurable 1-day, 7-day, and 30-day exports for estimated 5-hour, 7-day, and 30-day usage windows.
- Added optional official service-status monitoring for Codex, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Amp, with incident warnings shown on affected provider tabs.
- Added display preferences for used versus remaining quota and countdown versus exact local reset times.
- Added an option to show or hide the separate GPT-5.3 Spark rate-limit card reported by Codex.

## OpenCode Go

- OpenCode Console service-account keys can be stored in Windows Credential Manager, read from `OPENCODE_CONSOLE_SERVICE_API_KEY`, or held in memory for the current session.
- Standard OpenCode Go and Zen inference keys are rejected with a clear explanation because they cannot access billing exports.
- Local `opencode.db` history remains the fallback when no Console service-account key is configured.

## Updates and reliability

- Available app updates now appear in the main window, with download progress, confirmation prompts, and restart-to-update handling.
- Provider status checks use verified public endpoints, run only for enabled providers, and never send provider credentials.
- New settings are persisted per Windows user and invalid saved values safely fall back to defaults.

**Full changelog:** [v0.1.0...v0.2.0](https://github.com/CallMeLewis/UsageDeck/compare/v0.1.0...v0.2.0)
